response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong web/backend credentials but lacks the core C++ profile this role demands. C++ appears only briefly at National Instruments (~6 months in 2014-2015) and a bachelor thesis project; there is no evidence of recent modern C++/STL work, multi-threaded systems engineering, message brokers, MQTT/IoT, or IEC 62443/SDL exposure. Primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, which do not transfer cleanly to an industrial automation C++ architect role.
strengths: Broad senior engineering experience across web, backend, and embedded contexts
Strong testing/TDD culture and mentoring history (workshops, dojos, courses)
Familiarity with distributed/high-throughput systems and DevOps tooling
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience; no evidence of modern C++/STL architecture work
No exposure to industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.)
No demonstrated work with MQTT, message brokers, or IoT platforms required by the role

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL background
